description: Chunk-size independent retrieval evaluation set generated from the latest uploaded PDFs. Use this same file against 250, 350, 500, or other chunk sizes.

sourceFiles.title: Autonomous Ocean Drones for Coral Monitoring

scoringNotes.expectedChunkContains: Each inner array is one evidence unit. A retrieved chunk is relevant if it contains all strings in an evidence unit and matches the expected source when provided.
scoringNotes.single_hop: Pass Recall@K if any expected evidence unit appears in top K.
scoringNotes.multi_hop: Pass StrictMultiHopRecall if all expected evidence units appear in top K.
scoringNotes.precision: Relevant retrieved chunks divided by K.
scoringNotes.mrr: Reciprocal rank of the first relevant chunk.
scoringNotes.boundary: Passes when boundaryTermsMustBeTogether appear together in a retrieved chunk.
scoringNotes.no_answer: False positive if retriever returns confident evidence for a fact that is not present.

tests.question: What percentage reduction in water consumption was achieved by the Martian irrigation system?
expectedFacts: The irrigation system reduced water consumption by 37%.

tests.question: How much did tomato yields increase in the Martian greenhouse simulations?
expectedFacts: Tomato yields increased by 22%.

tests.question: Which university developed the quantum-enhanced irrigation system?
expectedFacts: The University of New Copenhagen developed the fictional quantum-enhanced irrigation system.

tests.question: Where were the Martian agriculture experiments conducted?
expectedFacts: The experiments were conducted inside the Borealis Dome in Iceland.

tests.question: What kind of soil was used in the Borealis Dome experiments?
expectedFacts: The experiments used simulated Martian regolith.

tests.question: Who led the Martian agriculture project?
expectedFacts: The project was led by Dr. Helena Sorensen.

tests.question: How many research institutions were involved in the Martian agriculture project?
expectedFacts: The project involved six research institutions.

tests.question: How often did moisture sensors collect readings?
expectedFacts: Moisture sensors collected readings every fifteen minutes.

tests.question: What did the quantum-assisted prediction model dynamically adjust?
expectedFacts: The model dynamically adjusted watering frequency and volume.

tests.question: What plant and environmental factors did researchers monitor during the Martian agriculture experiment?
expectedFacts: Researchers monitored plant health, biomass growth, nutrient retention, and environmental stability.

tests.question: How much funding did the Martian agriculture project receive?
expectedFacts: The project received €12 million from the Nordic Space Farming Initiative.

tests.question: What did researchers observe about predictive irrigation?
expectedFacts: Researchers observed that predictive irrigation reduced resource waste while maintaining crop consistency.

tests.question: Which crop experiment cut water use by more than one third?
expectedFacts: The Martian quantum-enhanced irrigation experiment reduced water consumption by 37%.

tests.question: What study used fake Martian soil inside an Icelandic dome?
expectedFacts: The Martian agriculture experiments used simulated Martian regolith inside the Borealis Dome in Iceland.

tests.question: Who led the study that reduced water consumption by 37%?
expectedFacts: The study reduced water consumption by 37%.
The project was led by Dr. Helena Sorensen.

tests.question: Which funding source supported the project conducted in the Borealis Dome?
expectedFacts: The experiments were conducted inside the Borealis Dome in Iceland.
The project received €12 million from the Nordic Space Farming Initiative.

tests.question: Who led the project that used simulated Martian regolith?
expectedFacts: The project used simulated Martian regolith and was led by Dr. Helena Sorensen.

tests.question: What model adjusted watering frequency and volume?
expectedFacts: A quantum-assisted prediction model dynamically adjusted watering frequency and volume.

tests.question: What was the name of the coral monitoring drone network?
expectedFacts: The drone network was called CoralEye.

tests.question: How many Pacific Ocean locations did CoralEye monitor?
expectedFacts: CoralEye monitored reef health across 42 Pacific Ocean locations.

tests.question: How much earlier did the CoralEye system detect coral bleaching events?
expectedFacts: The system detected bleaching events 17 days earlier than traditional survey approaches.

tests.question: What kind of image analysis did CoralEye combine with autonomous sensing?
expectedFacts: CoralEye combined autonomous sensing with machine-learning-based image analysis.

tests.question: Why are traditional coral monitoring approaches difficult?
expectedFacts: Traditional monitoring often requires extensive fieldwork, specialized equipment, and substantial travel costs.

tests.question: What power system did each drone use?
expectedFacts: Each drone used a hybrid solar-wave power system.

tests.question: How long could each drone operate continuously?
expectedFacts: Each drone could operate continuously for up to 11 months.

tests.question: What types of data did the CoralEye drones capture?
expectedFacts: The drones captured high-resolution imagery, environmental measurements, and reef health indicators.

tests.question: Where was CoralEye data transmitted for processing?
expectedFacts: Data was transmitted to centralized processing systems.

tests.question: What image-classification accuracy did ReefNet-V5 achieve?
expectedFacts: ReefNet-V5 achieved an image-classification accuracy of 94.8%.

tests.question: What did persistent monitoring enable in the coral drone study?
expectedFacts: Persistent monitoring enabled earlier detection of bleaching events and provided long-term trend visibility.

tests.question: What improvements did researchers expect from autonomous marine sensing networks?
expectedFacts: Researchers concluded autonomous marine sensing networks could improve response times, increase observational coverage, and reduce dependence on infrequent manual surveys.

tests.question: Which marine monitoring system gave researchers more than two weeks of extra warning?
expectedFacts: The CoralEye drone network detected coral bleaching events 17 days earlier.

tests.question: What autonomous reef project tried to reduce dependence on manual surveys?
expectedFacts: The CoralEye initiative explored autonomous systems and researchers concluded they could reduce dependence on infrequent manual surveys.

tests.question: How long could the drones operate and what image-classification accuracy did ReefNet-V5 achieve?
expectedFacts: Drones could operate for up to 11 months.
ReefNet-V5 achieved 94.8% image-classification accuracy.

tests.question: What power system supported the drones and what data did they capture?
expectedFacts: Each drone used a hybrid solar-wave power system.
The drones captured high-resolution imagery, environmental measurements, and reef health indicators.

tests.question: Which power system enabled operation for up to 11 months?
expectedFacts: A hybrid solar-wave power system enabled operation for up to 11 months.

tests.question: Which model achieved 94.8% accuracy?
expectedFacts: ReefNet-V5 achieved 94.8% image-classification accuracy.

tests.question: What was the name of the AI-assisted battery discovery platform?
expectedFacts: The platform was called ElectroMind.

tests.question: How many candidate battery compounds did ElectroMind evaluate?
expectedFacts: ElectroMind evaluated 8.2 million candidate battery compounds.

tests.question: What was the goal of the ElectroMind project?
expectedFacts: The project aimed to accelerate materials discovery by reducing exhaustive laboratory screening.

tests.question: What battery research process is traditionally time-consuming?
expectedFacts: Discovering new battery chemistries is traditionally time-consuming and involves years of experimentation and validation.

tests.question: What techniques did ElectroMind combine to identify promising compounds?
expectedFacts: ElectroMind combined predictive modeling, chemical simulation, and ranking algorithms.

tests.question: How much electricity did ElectroMind training consume?
expectedFacts: Training consumed approximately 3.1 GWh of electricity.

tests.question: How long did ElectroMind training last?
expectedFacts: Training lasted over a nine-month period.

tests.question: What did the ElectroMind platform do after analyzing candidate materials?
expectedFacts: The platform generated performance estimates and prioritized compounds for laboratory testing.

tests.question: What battery chemistry did the system identify?
expectedFacts: The system identified a lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry.

tests.question: How much did the lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry increase energy density?
expectedFacts: The lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry increased energy density by 28%.

tests.question: Where were the final validation experiments conducted?
expectedFacts: Final validation experiments were conducted at the Zurich Energy Laboratory in Switzerland.

tests.question: What did researchers conclude about AI-driven discovery platforms?
expectedFacts: Researchers concluded that AI-driven discovery platforms could shorten development cycles and improve efficiency in advanced materials research.

tests.question: Which battery formulation improved storage density by nearly thirty percent?
expectedFacts: A lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry increased energy density by 28%.

tests.question: What AI system screened millions of battery materials before lab testing?
expectedFacts: ElectroMind evaluated 8.2 million candidate battery compounds and prioritized compounds for laboratory testing.

tests.question: How many compounds were evaluated and how much electricity did training consume?
expectedFacts: ElectroMind evaluated 8.2 million candidate battery compounds.
Training consumed approximately 3.1 GWh of electricity.

tests.question: Which chemistry improved energy density and where was it validated?
expectedFacts: A lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry increased energy density by 28%.
Final validation experiments were conducted at the Zurich Energy Laboratory in Switzerland.

tests.question: Which chemistry increased energy density by 28%?
expectedFacts: The lithium-sodium hybrid chemistry increased energy density by 28%.

tests.question: Where were the final validation experiments for the 28% energy-density result conducted?
expectedFacts: The 28% energy-density result was validated at the Zurich Energy Laboratory in Switzerland.

tests.question: Which paper involved Iceland and which involved Switzerland?
expectedFacts: The Martian agriculture paper involved Iceland.
The battery discovery paper involved Switzerland.

tests.question: Compare the main percentage improvements in the Martian agriculture and battery discovery papers.
expectedFacts: The Martian agriculture paper reported 37% water savings and 22% tomato yield improvement.
The battery paper reported a 28% energy-density improvement.

tests.question: Which system operated for 11 months and which system trained for nine months?
expectedFacts: The CoralEye drones operated for up to 11 months.
ElectroMind training lasted a nine-month period.

tests.question: Which project used a hybrid solar-wave power system and which used a quantum-assisted prediction model?
expectedFacts: The coral drone project used a hybrid solar-wave power system.
The Martian agriculture project used a quantum-assisted prediction model.

tests.question: Which project focused on reef health and which focused on battery materials discovery?
expectedFacts: The CoralEye project focused on reef health.
The ElectroMind project focused on battery materials discovery.

tests.question: Which paper mentioned 42 locations and which mentioned six research institutions?
expectedFacts: The coral drone paper mentioned 42 Pacific Ocean locations.
The Martian agriculture paper mentioned six research institutions.
